title: Alphonse de Polignac
content: Alphonse de Polignac (1826–1863) was a French mathematician. In 1849, the year he was admitted to Polytechnique, he made what's known as Polignac's conjecture: The case "k" = 1 is the twin prime conjecture. He also conjectured Romanov's theorem. His father, Jules de Polignac (1780-1847) was prime minister of Charles X until the Bourbon dynasty was overthrown (1830).
